Nuclei density, determined by real part of index of
Two coherent beams traverse two different
path lengths in a gaseous sample of the material
to be studied. This causes a phase shift between
the two beams. By forcing these two beams to
interfere this phase shift can be determined from
the beam intensity oscillations as a function of
the density (i.e. the pressure, provided the
temperature is monitored) of the sample. For
more details and for formulas see Z.Physik,271,177.

but had noticeable influence for He-3 only.
